issues.title: chore: archive pygame-era debris, fix PEP 701 stragglers + broken workflows
issues.body:
## Summary

CI cleanup that gets the Python 3.11 baseline gate to actually pass across the
whole automation tree, archives genuinely-broken pygame-era debris, and repairs
two workflows that had been failing at startup since April 2026.

## What changed

### 1. Archived broken pygame-era debris (29 files)
- 28 root `tests/` and `tools/` files with **genuine syntax errors on every
  Python version** (verified failing on 3.10 and 3.13) moved to
  `archive/legacy-pygame/` preserving their relative subpath. Confirmed via grep
  that none are imported by live code (`godot/`, `scripts/`, remaining `tools/`)
  before moving.
- +1 more (`tools/temp_intelligence_dialog.py`, see below) = **29 archived total**.
- Fixed the pre-commit `exclude` regex, which only matched `archived/` and
  `docs/archive/` — so the real top-level `archive/`/`legacy/` trees were still
  being linted (and the crude `check-pygame-dir` hook false-positived on
  `legacy-pygame/` paths). Added `archive/` and `legacy/`.

### 2. Fixed 12 "version-only" PEP 701 f-string files
Eleven compiled only on 3.13 (nested same-quote f-strings) and failed the 3.11
baseline. Flipped each offending outer single quote to double (no file contains a
literal `"`, so the result is legal on Python 3.9+) — same pattern as #527.
`test_upgrades.py` also had two non-f-string nested-quote bugs (broken on all
versions) fixed identically. Repaired the latent lint debt the syntax fix exposed
(E402 `# noqa` on intentional post-`sys.path` imports, removed F841 unused locals,
E712 `== True` → `is True`).

**The 12th, `tools/temp_intelligence_dialog.py`, was archived instead of fixed:**
it is dead pygame-era code (references `pygame` ~20x with no `import pygame`, uses
an undefined `wrap_text`), so it never ran. Reconstructing it is not a cheap/safe
quote-flip, so it went to `archive/legacy-pygame/`.

**Legacy flags for a later archive pass (decision left to maintainer):**
- *Dead — import the now-archived pygame `src/` tree, so cannot run in the live
  tree:* `tests/test_action_points.py`, `tests/test_economic_cycles.py`,
  `tests/test_ui_fixes.py`, `tests/test_ui_transitions.py`, `tests/test_upgrades.py`
  (also had all-version syntax bugs), `tools/dev/demo_technical_failures.py`.
- *Likely stale:* `tools/integration_test_v0_10_0.py` (pinned to v0.10.0; current
  is v0.11.0).
- *Probably keep (self-contained automation / doc examples):*
  `docs/shared/data-batch-integration.py`, `docs/shared/website-version-api.py`,
  `tools/cleanup-duplicate-issues.py`, `tools/github-issue-sync.py`.

### 3. Repaired 2 startup-broken workflows
`data-validation.yml` and `dev-blog-automation.yml` failed at startup (0s,
"workflow file issue") on every push since April 2026.

**Root cause (both):** embedded scripts had multi-line string bodies written
flush-left (column 0). Inside a YAML block scalar (`run:`/`script: |`), a line
indented below the scalar's base indent terminates the scalar, so GitHub rejected
the files as invalid YAML at parse time. **Fix:** raise those string-body lines up
to the block base indent (12 for the data-validation github-script template
literal, 8 for the dev-blog Python heredocs). YAML strips the base indent back
off, so the embedded JS/Python is **byte-identical after stripping** (verified:
both heredocs still compile and rendered strings stay flush-left). Also modernized
the deprecated `::set-output` → `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` (heredoc-delimiter form for safe
multi-line values) and dropped the `check-yaml` pre-commit exclusion that was
masking these two files.

**Verified on this branch (workflow_dispatch):** both now get **past
startup_failure**.
- `data-validation`: "Validate Historical Data" job **passes**; the schema-health
  job fails only later at the AJV `--strict=true` JSON-schema check (pre-existing
  content issue).
- `dev-blog`: runs into the Python step and fails at `repo.iter_commits('main')`
  (git exit 128) — only because the dispatch ran off `chore/ci-cleanup` where the
  `main` ref isn't checked out; on its real push-to-`main` trigger that ref exists.

Both remaining failures are runtime/content issues, not startup failures.

### 4. Widened the CI syntax gate
`quality-checks.yml`'s "Python syntax gate (3.11 baseline)" compiled only
`scripts/*.py`. Extended it to `tools/`, `tests/`, and `docs/shared/` (via `find`,
excluding nested `archive/`/`legacy/`), now that those trees are clean.

## Verification
`git ls-files '*.py' | grep -vE '^(archive|legacy)/'` → **137 files, 0 compile
failures** on Python 3.10 (strict stand-in for the 3.11 baseline). The widened
gate's own `find` set is 128 files, all clean.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Insight System (Skill-Gated Visibility)
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement an insight system that gates player visibility into game mechanics based on character skills.

**Related to**: Risk System design (see `godot/docs/design/RISK_SYSTEM.md`)

---

## Feature Description

Player insight into risk, consequences, and NPC reactions scales with **Insight** skills. Inspired by Civilization's diplomacy system where higher relationship/intel reveals more about AI intentions.

### Insight Levels

| Level | What Player Sees |
|-------|------------------|
| **None (0)** | Outcomes only, no explanations |
| **Low (1-2)** | Vague hints: "Reaction will likely be mixed" |
| **Medium (3-4)** | Directional: "This will increase regulatory scrutiny" |
| **High (5-6)** | Specific: "OpenPhil dislikes this; Anthropic neutral" |
| **Expert (7+)** | Quantified: "Regulatory Attention +8, Financial Exposure +3" |

### Insight Domains

Different skills reveal different aspects:
- **Political Insight** → Regulatory Attention, Public Awareness visibility
- **Financial Insight** → Financial Exposure visibility  
- **Academic Insight** → Research Integrity, Capability Overhang visibility
- **Organizational Insight** → Insider Threat visibility

### Acquisition

Insight can be developed through:
- Character creation (allocate points at game start)
- Upgrades and investments during play
- Experience in specific domains (repeated interactions)

---

## Design Notes

From design discussion (2025-12-25):
> "Players can have relative insight into the precision of the impacts of their plays by gaining skill in those kinds of interactions over time... player's Insight level vary from hints 'reaction to this will likely be mixed' to specific 'OpenPhil will seriously dislike this, Anthropic will be neutral' similar to advanced diplomacy giving you more insight into things in the game of Civilization."

Key principle: Some players prefer operating with limited information (challenge), others want full visibility (strategy optimization). The system accommodates both playstyles.

---

## Implementation Notes

### Phase 1 (Current - Risk System)
- Dev mode toggle: full visibility on/off
- No skill gating yet

### Phase 2 (This Issue)
- Integrate with character creation
- Implement skill-based visibility filtering
- Add upgrade paths for insight skills

---

## Integration Points

- **Risk System**: Insight determines how much risk pool info is shown
- **Character Creation**: Insight skills allocated at game start
- **Events**: Causal explanations gated by insight level
- **Actions**: Pre-action feedback ("This will likely...") gated by insight
- **NPCs/Funders**: Relationship visibility gated by political insight

---

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Insight levels affect displayed information
- [ ] At least 4 insight domains implemented
- [ ] Character creation integration
- [ ] Upgrade path for insight skills
- [ ] Graceful degradation (low insight still playable)
- [ ] Dev mode bypasses insight gating

issues.title: Revisit terminal/CRT texture aesthetic (parked art package)
issues.body:
## What this is
A never-merged **visual direction** for the game UI: a retro **terminal / CRT** aesthetic plus a library of **institutional surface textures**, with `theme_manager` integration and several menus already retextured. Parked during the June 2026 branch cleanup so it isn't lost — to be revisited when we next work on UI/UX.

## Why parked, not merged
It's an art-direction call, not a bug/feature — worth a deliberate "do we want this look?" decision while actively doing UI work, rather than merging blind or dropping.

## What's in the package (~20 PNG textures + generation prompts)
- `godot/assets/textures/surfaces/` — bakelite (cracked), concrete (institutional), CRT burn-in, grid (circuit-trace / aged graphpaper / perforated metal), linoleum (damaged), oxidized copper, painted metal panel, plywood (stained)
- `godot/assets/textures/terminal/` — amber (noise/scanlines), blue (BSOD/DOS bg), cyan (border), green (grid/scanlines), etc.
- `art_prompts/batch_4_terminal_textures.yaml` — the prompts used to generate them (so the set is reproducible/extendable)
- `godot/autoload/theme_manager.gd` — integration hooks
- Retextured scenes: `welcome`, `settings_menu`, `pregame_setup`, `leaderboard_screen`, `end_game_screen`
- `godot/scripts/ui/doom_meter.gd` — minor texture tweak

## How to recover / preview
- Permanent tag (survives branch cleanup): `git checkout parked/ui-crt-textures`
- Original branch tip: `feature/ui-texture-integration` @ `5c0b74a` (3 commits, +1200 lines)
- To see it live: rebase the tag onto current `main` and run in Godot.

## When picking this up
- Decide if the CRT/terminal look fits the current UI direction.
- Even if not adopting wholesale, the texture set + `art_prompts` yaml are reusable — cherry-pick individual surfaces/overlays.

_Parked from `feature/ui-texture-integration`; assets preserved at tag `parked/ui-crt-textures`._

issues.title: Lab Ledger: player-facing review page with insight-gated 'redacted' risk zone
issues.body:
## Summary
A player-facing **Lab Ledger / Briefing** page where players can review and understand the constant/passive effects acting on them — the Civilization analogue of drilling into your upkeep budget. Raised during #500 (Research Quality) design.

Design captured in `godot/docs/design/TWO_ACT_STRUCTURE.md` (section "Player-facing review: the Ledger & the Redacted zone").

## Two zones
- **Known Effects (transparent):** every active passive modifier the player owns or can see — research-quality stance (speed multiplier + its risk deltas), upgrade passives, per-researcher productivity/doom contributions, per-turn research & upkeep ("budget" drill-down), visible doom sources.
- **Redacted Effects (insight-gated):** the 6 hidden risk pools, rendered as redacted/obscured entries (████) the player knows *exist* but cannot read — the "things affecting you that you're oblivious to" zone. This is the UI realisation of the Insight / Situational-Awareness system, reading straight off the existing `risk_pool.get_narrative_hint(pool, insight_level)` ladder (vague → directional → specific → quantified).

## Initial release
Insight is a **dev-mode toggle** (0 = fully redacted normally; 7+ under F3), per `RISK_SYSTEM.md` Player Visibility section. Skill-gated insight is future work (needs character creation).

## Dependency
The UI receives state as dicts, not the live object. Add a `GameManager.get_risk_hints(insight_level)` accessor wrapping `state.risk_system.get_all_narrative_hints(...)` (and/or `get_dev_mode_data()`).

## Related
- #500 (Research Quality) — first passive system the Ledger would surface
- `RISK_SYSTEM.md`, `TWO_ACT_STRUCTURE.md`

Reusable by every future passive-effect system, so worth its own component rather than bolting onto #500.

issues.title: Track down the CSIRO/Data61 retro Win3-style web UI project
issues.body:
Find the CSIRO or Data61 (or AU collaborator) project that wrapped a modern web UI in a very square/boxy 1990s Windows-3 aesthetic — possibly a game-dev effort. Wanted specifically because its output would be CC-licensed and reusable in pdoom1's bureaucratic UI. Prior searches failed; closest hits were Ash Kyd's ui95 (not CSIRO) and modern (non-retro) AU gov design systems.

_Captured from distilled conversation backlog (src 39)._

issues.title: chore: Clean up GDScript warnings and style issues
issues.body:
## Summary
During QA testing, a number of GDScript warnings were observed in the Godot debugger console. These don't cause runtime errors but should be cleaned up for code quality.

## Warnings to address
From the debugger output:

- **Integer division warnings** - Multiple instances of `Integer division. Decimal part will be discarded.`
- **Unused local variables** - Variables declared but never used:
  - `event_type`
  - `start_result`
  - `turn_result`
  - `event_id`
  - `research_label` (shadowing)
  - `current_money`
  - `panel`
- **Ternary operator type mismatch** - `Values of the ternary operator are not mutually compatible`
- **Unused parameter** - `doom_percentage` in `update_doom_level()`
- **Iterator naming conflict** - `seed` iterator has same name as built-in function

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All GDScript warnings resolved or intentionally suppressed with comments
- [ ] No new warnings introduced
- [ ] Game still functions correctly after changes

## Notes
These are style/quality issues only - the game runs fine with these warnings present.

issues.title: QA: Test Doom System & Victory Conditions
issues.body:
## QA Testing: Doom System & Victory Conditions

Work through the **Doom System** and **Victory Conditions** sections of `QA_CHECKLIST.md`.

### What to test
- Doom increases with unsafe actions, decreases with safety research
- Doom velocity and momentum in debug overlay
- Doom status text accuracy (Stable, Rising, etc.)
- Game over at Doom 100
- Victory condition tracking and progress display
- Victory screen on win

### How to run
1. Clone the repo and open `godot/project.godot` in Godot 4.5.1
2. Press F5 to run, use debug overlay (~) to monitor doom values
3. Use debug controls to accelerate testing (add money, trigger events)
4. See `CONTRIBUTING.md` for full setup instructions

### How to report
- Comment on this issue with a summary of items passed/failed/untestable
- For each bug found, create a separate issue using the **QA Bug Report** template

issues.title: QA: Test Accessibility Features section
issues.body:
## QA Testing: Accessibility Features

Work through the **Accessibility Features** section of `QA_CHECKLIST.md` and report results.

### What to test
- Colorblind mode toggle in Settings > Accessibility
- Protanopia, Deuteranopia, Tritanopia modes
- High contrast mode
- Keyboard navigation (all buttons focusable, focus indicators visible, shortcuts on tooltips)

### How to run
1. Clone the repo and open `godot/project.godot` in Godot 4.5.1
2. Press F5 to run the game
3. Press F10 to open Settings, navigate to Accessibility
4. See `CONTRIBUTING.md` for full setup instructions

### How to report
- Comment on this issue with a summary of items passed/failed/untestable
- For each bug found, create a separate issue using the **QA Bug Report** template
- Screenshots are especially helpful for visual accessibility issues

issues.title: QA: Test Events System section
issues.body:
## QA Testing: Events System

Work through the **Events System** section of `QA_CHECKLIST.md` and report results.

### What to test
- Random event triggering during gameplay
- Event popup display (title, description, choices)
- Event choice effects and resolution logging
- Debug event testing (trigger specific events via debug overlay)
  - Media coverage, funding, research breakthrough, crisis/disaster events

### How to run
1. Clone the repo and open `godot/project.godot` in Godot 4.5.1
2. Press F5 to run the game
3. Play several turns to trigger events naturally, or use debug overlay (~) to trigger manually
4. See `CONTRIBUTING.md` for full setup instructions

### How to report
- Comment on this issue with a summary of items passed/failed/untestable
- For each bug found, create a separate issue using the **QA Bug Report** template

issues.title: QA: Test Debug & Developer Tools section
issues.body:
## QA Testing: Debug & Developer Tools

Work through the **Debug & Developer Tools** section of `QA_CHECKLIST.md` and report results.

### What to test
- Debug overlay toggle (~)
- Game State tab (turn, resources, doom, staff, actions, events, rivals)
- Errors tab
- Performance tab (FPS, frame time, memory, objects, draw calls)
- Debug Controls tab (add money, add AP, trigger events, reset, refresh slider)

### How to run
1. Clone the repo and open `godot/project.godot` in Godot 4.5.1
2. Press F5 to run the game
3. Press ~ to open the debug overlay
4. See `CONTRIBUTING.md` for full setup instructions

### How to report
- Comment on this issue with a summary of items passed/failed/untestable
- For each bug found, create a separate issue using the **QA Bug Report** template

issues.title: QA: Test Core Gameplay Flow section
issues.body:
## QA Testing: Core Gameplay Flow

Work through the **Core Gameplay Flow** section of `QA_CHECKLIST.md` and report results.

### What to test
- Turn 1 basic actions (new game, resources, AP, doom meter)
- Hiring staff (H key, queue actions, turn preview)
- Action queue management (undo Z, clear C)
- Executing turns (Space, resource updates, turn counter, message log)
- Quick menu hotkeys (H, F, R, P, T, Z, C, Space, Esc, Tab, Shift+Tab)

### How to run
1. Clone the repo and open `godot/project.godot` in Godot 4.5.1
2. Press F5 to run the game
3. See `CONTRIBUTING.md` for full setup instructions

### How to report
- Comment on this issue with a summary of items passed/failed/untestable
- For each bug found, create a separate issue using the **QA Bug Report** template
- Include screenshots where helpful (press `[` in-game to take a screenshot)

Public Opinion & Media System

Missing: Dynamic public sentiment tracking
Opportunity: Media cycles, public scandals, PR campaigns
Integration: Reputation could affect funding, recruitment, regulatory pressure

issues.title: (0.2) Enhancement - regulations

milestone.title: Future Releases

issues.body:
Regulatory/Government Mechanics

Underexplored: While competitors have "regulatory influence," player interaction with regulators seems limited
Opportunity: Lobbying mini-games, compliance costs, regulatory capture mechanics
Stakes: Regulations could cap competitor progress or unlock new research paths

Depth: Some labs might relocate to more permissive jurisdictions
